description.value:
Starting at 3rd level, you gain access to a special weapon from a unique line, specially made to be wielded by YoRHa. You are always proficient in this weapon. Additionally, it cannot be broken, you cannot be disarmed of it, and it takes the form of a melee weapon of your choice.

While you are not wielding this weapon, it floats next to you, and you can swing it without physically holding it. If it would ever leave your side, it returns to float in an open space within 5 feet of you at the start of your next turn.

Regardless of form, this weapon's damage die is 1d10, and it can use your Dexterity or Intelligence modifier for weapon attacks and for the requirements of any properties.

This damage die increases by one step at 7th level. At 11th level, they treat 1s and 2s on their damage die as 3s. At 15th level, they roll their damage die twice, taking the higher result. YoRHa weapons can trigger the damage of the Bruised condition when they deal damage, regardless of their damage type.

When you gain this feature, choose between Virtue and Cruelty. When you do, you gain effects based on your choice:

Virtue:

While you are not bloodied, you gain the following benefits:

  • You add your full proficiency bonus to your AC instead of half.

  • Your movement speed increases by an amount of feet equal to 5 times your proficiency bonus.

  • Weapon attacks with your YoRHa weapon and jutsu cast with it as a component add your proficiency bonus to their damage.

  • At 7th level, hostile creatures that you critically hit with your YoRHa weapon twice in the same turn become Dazed and Concussed.

  • At 11th level, you ignore half of the damage reduction of creatures you critically hit with your YoRHa weapon.

Cruelty:

While you are bloodied, you gain the following benefits:

  • You gain damage reduction equal to your proficiency bonus. If you are wearing heavy armor, this is halved.

  • You can Dodge as a Bonus Action. At 11th level, you can spend 5 chakra to instead Dodge as a free action.

  • Weapon attacks with your YoRHa weapon and jutsu cast with it as a component deal an additional die of damage.

  • At 7th level, hostile creatures that you critically hit twice in the same turn become Weakened and Lacerated.

  • At 11th level, you ignore half of the damage reduction of creatures you critically hit with your YoRHa weapon.
